name: Chocolate-Dipped Biscotti
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Unsalted Butter, melted and cooled
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir together the sugar and melted butter.
2. Add liqueur, whiskey, and anise seed to the mixture. Beat in eggs, then stir in the nuts.
3. Sift the dry ingredients (flour and baking powder) into the wet ingredients until well incorporated.
4. Cover with plastic wrap and chill for about 3 hours.
5. Preheat oven to 375F.
6. Butter two cookie sheets.
7. Shape the dough into three loaves on the prepared cookie sheets, spacing them evenly. Each loaf should be about 2 inches wide and 1/2 inch thick.
8. Bake for about 20 minutes or until the loaves are puffed and lightly browned.
9. Allow the loaves to cool completely.
10. Cut each loaf into diagonal slices about 1/2 inch thick.
11. Arrange the slices on their cut sides with the cut side facing up.
12. Toast the slices in the oven at 375F for an additional 15 minutes or until lightly browned.
13. Cool thoroughly.
14. Melt chocolate chips and vegetable shortening together in a double boiler, stirring frequently until the mixture reaches 85F on a candy thermometer.
15. Dip each biscotti into the melted chocolate, making sure to cover the tops completely.
16. Immediately turn the cookies so that the uncoated side is facing down on wax paper.
17. Repeat dipping process for all biscuits.
